headline: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
description: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

headline: Russian drone crash in Romania: NATO still seeks defense systems adapted to drone incursions
US judge freezes Trump 'slush fund' for allies
Who will manage to capture the 'middle' electorate Macron seized 10 years ago?
description: Among French President Emmanuel Macron's political heirs, and even on the left, some are starting to believe that the presidential campaign could play out in the middle, amid the collapse of France's former governing parties.

headline: Climate change suspected of contributing to recent surge in hantavirus cases in Argentina
description: During the 2025-2026 summer season, 106 cases of hantavirus infection were reported in Argentina, nearly double the previous year, and at much higher levels than in the past.

headline: Lior Amihai, director of Israeli NGO Peace Now: 'The coalition in power wants to annex the territories in the West Bank and establish a racist state based on Jewish supremacy'
description: Driven by a messianic ideology, Benjamin Netanyahu's government has bureaucratized the expansion of West Bank settlements since late 2022. But according to the director of a major peace advocacy group, the majority of Israeli public opinion does not support this policy.

headline: Europe's heatwave in pictures, from Milan's Duomo to the crowded beaches of southern England
description: Europe is currently experiencing an unprecedented heatwave. From Italy to the United Kingdom, several countries have recorded temperatures far above seasonal norms, prompting some authorities to issue the highest-level health alerts.

38° at 11 am. French students and staff struggle with unbearable classroom temperatures
description: Across France, primary, middle and high schools have reported the severity of the situation in their overheated classrooms. Education professionals are criticizing the lack of response in recent years from both local authorities, who are responsible for school buildings, and from the Education Ministry, in charge of the safety of students and staff.

headline: How China uses influencers to polish its image
description: Chongqing, a futuristic Chinese city, has caught the eye of influencers and bloggers. Beijing, meanwhile, welcomes new visitors, often unaware of the geopolitical and ideological currents running beneath their social media posts.

headline: 'Do you realize that your statements are completely absurd?': Inside the arrest of the Louvre burglars
description: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.
byline: Jean-Michel Décugis,  Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Nicolas-Charles Torrent
